msserver2019standard.label: Windows Server 2019
msserver2019standard.action: Apply cumulative update KB5094123 via WSUS or Windows Update (supersedes KB5087538)

alternatives.label: May 2026 CU
alternatives.action: Apply May 2026 cumulative update as a rollback target if June CU causes issues
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Pinning to an older CU leaves June 2026 CVEs unpatched. Use only as a temporary rollback.

alternatives.label: WSUS Catalog
alternatives.action: Approve and deploy via internal WSUS server according to change management process

msserver2012r2standard.label: Windows Server 2012 R2
msserver2012r2standard.action: Apply Monthly Rollup KB5094041 via WSUS (ESU license required; SSU KB5079233 prereq)
msserver2012r2standard.warning: ⚠️ End of Support Oct 2023. ESU required. Final EOL: October 13, 2026 — plan migration.

alternatives.label: Security-Only
alternatives.action: Look up the June 2026 Security-Only update for Server 2012 R2 in Update Catalog (ESU required); apply for minimal change footprint
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Security-only skips reliability/quality fixes in the Monthly Rollup. EOL: October 13, 2026 — plan migration to Server 2022.

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2022
alternatives.action: Plan in-place upgrade or migration to Windows Server 2022 before October 2026 EOL
alternatives.warning: 🚫 Final EOL: October 13, 2026. No further patches after that date even with ESU. Migration is the only long-term fix.

msserver2016standard.label: Windows Server 2016
msserver2016standard.action: Apply cumulative update KB5094122 via WSUS or Windows Update (supersedes KB5087537)
msserver2016standard.warning: ⚠️ Server 2016 mainstream support ends January 2027. Begin migration planning to Server 2022 now.

alternatives.label: May 2026 CU
alternatives.action: Apply May 2026 cumulative update as a rollback target if June CU causes issues
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Pinning to an older CU leaves June 2026 CVEs unpatched. Use only as a temporary rollback.

alternatives.label: WSUS Catalog
alternatives.action: Approve and deploy KB5094122 via internal WSUS server (approve SSU KB5088064 first)

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2022
alternatives.action: Consider migration to Windows Server 2022 (mainstream support until Oct 2031)
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Server 2016 mainstream support ends January 2027. Begin migration planning now.

msserver2022standard.label: Windows Server 2022
msserver2022standard.action: Apply cumulative update KB5094128 via WSUS or Windows Update (supersedes KB5087545)

alternatives.label: WSUS Catalog
alternatives.action: Approve and deploy KB5094128 via internal WSUS server (approve SSU KB5089140 first)

alternatives.label: May 2026 CU
alternatives.action: Apply May 2026 cumulative update as a rollback target if June CU causes issues
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Pinning to an older CU leaves June 2026 CVEs unpatched. Use only as a temporary rollback.

.NET Framework (All Versions).label: .NET Framework
.NET Framework (All Versions).action: Apply May 2026 .NET 4.8.1 cumulative update (KB varies by host OS — see release notes table) via Windows Update or WSUS
.NET Framework (All Versions).warning: ⚠️ Addresses CVE-2026-32177 (.NET Framework Elevation of Privilege). 4.8.1 is the final .NET Framework release. | Reviewed Jun 10, 2026: no June 2026 update from Microsoft this cycle — May 2026 KBs remain current.

alternatives.label: Win Server 2022
alternatives.action: Apply KB5087059 (.NET 3.5 + 4.8.1 cumulative for Server 2022)

alternatives.label: Win Server 2012 R2
alternatives.action: Apply Security and Quality Rollup KB5088861 for .NET 3.5 / 4.6.2 / 4.7.x / 4.8 on Server 2012 R2 (ESU required)
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Server 2012 R2 requires ESU. Final EOL October 2026.

alternatives.label: Migrate → .NET 8
alternatives.action: Migrate applications to .NET 8 LTS for modern runtime support and future security updates
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ .NET Framework 4.x is legacy. New features and performance improvements are only in .NET 5+.

SQL Server 2019 (All Components).label: SQL Server 2019
SQL Server 2019 (All Components).action: Install CU32 + GDR (KB5090407). CU32 is the final CU; May 2026 security GDR applied on top.
SQL Server 2019 (All Components).warning: ⚠️ Mainstream Support ended Feb 28, 2025. Extended Support until Jan 8, 2030. | Reviewed Jun 10, 2026: no June 2026 update from Microsoft this cycle — May 2026 KBs remain current.

alternatives.label: GDR Only
alternatives.action: Apply GDR-only patch for security fixes without cumulative update changes (lower change risk)
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ GDR-only skips reliability and feature fixes in CU32. Use if CU testing is not yet complete.

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2022
alternatives.action: Plan upgrade to SQL Server 2022 (mainstream support until Jan 2033)
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Mainstream Support ended Feb 2025. Begin planning upgrade to SQL Server 2022.

SQL Server 2022 (All Components).label: SQL Server 2022
SQL Server 2022 (All Components).action: Install CU24 + GDR (KB5089900, build 16.0.4252.3). Apply CU24 first if on CU23 or lower, then this May 2026 security update layers the GDR fixes on top. Do not mix CU and GDR branches.
SQL Server 2022 (All Components).warning: ℹ️ Mainstream support until Jan 11, 2028; Extended until Jan 11, 2033. CU25 (KB5081477, build 16.0.4255.1) shipped May 20, 2026 with no new security fixes beyond CU24+GDR — plan to move to CU25 in next maintenance window. Reviewed Jun 10, 2026: no June 2026 update from Microsoft this cycle — May KBs remain current.

alternatives.label: GDR Only
alternatives.action: Install GDR-only KB5091158 (build 16.0.1180.1) for security fixes without CU content. Lower change risk.
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ GDR-only skips CU24 reliability and feature improvements. CU24 + GDR is recommended for most environments.

alternatives.label: Apr 2026 CU
alternatives.action: Apply April 2026 CU24+GDR (KB5083252) as a rollback target if May CU+GDR causes issues.
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Pinning to an older CU leaves May 2026 CVEs unpatched. Use only as a temporary rollback.

SQL Server 2025 (All Components).label: SQL Server 2025
SQL Server 2025 (All Components).action: Install CU4 + GDR (KB5089899, build 17.0.4040.1). Apply CU4 first if not yet on CU4, then layer the May 2026 GDR via this package. Do not mix CU and GDR branches.
SQL Server 2025 (All Components).warning: ℹ️ SQL Server 2025 is the current major release (GA Nov 2025) — well within mainstream support. CU5 (KB5084896, build 17.0.4045.5) shipped May 20, 2026 with no new May security fixes beyond CU4+GDR. Young product on monthly CU cadence — higher regression risk, test in non-prod first. Reviewed Jun 10, 2026: no June 2026 update from Microsoft this cycle — May KBs remain current.

alternatives.label: GDR Only
alternatives.action: Install GDR-only KB5091223 (build 17.0.1115.1) for security fixes without CU content. Lower change risk.
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ GDR-only skips CU4 reliability and feature improvements. CU+GDR is recommended for most environments.

alternatives.label: Apr 2026 CU
alternatives.action: Apply April 2026 CU3+GDR (KB5083245) as a rollback target if May CU+GDR causes issues.
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Pinning to an older CU leaves May 2026 CVEs unpatched. Use only as a temporary rollback.

SQL Server 2017 (All Components).label: SQL Server 2017
SQL Server 2017 (All Components).action: Install CU31 + GDR (KB5090354). May 2026 security GDR on top of final CU.
SQL Server 2017 (All Components).warning: ⚠️ Mainstream Support ended Oct 2022. Extended Support until Oct 2027. | Reviewed Jun 10, 2026: no June 2026 update from Microsoft this cycle — May 2026 KBs remain current.

alternatives.label: GDR Only
alternatives.action: Apply GDR-only patch for security fixes with minimal change impact
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ GDR-only skips CU31 reliability and feature fixes. CU31 + GDR is recommended.

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2022
alternatives.action: Plan upgrade to SQL Server 2022 — mainstream support until January 2033
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Extended Support ends Oct 2027. Upgrade planning should begin now.

SQL Server 2016 (All Components).label: SQL Server 2016
SQL Server 2016 (All Components).action: Install SP3 + GDR (KB5089271). No further CUs will be released for SQL Server 2016.
SQL Server 2016 (All Components).warning: ⚠️ Mainstream Support ended July 2021. Extended Support ends July 11, 2026 — migration is urgent. | Reviewed Jun 10, 2026: no June 2026 update from Microsoft this cycle — May 2026 KBs remain current.

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2022
alternatives.action: Plan upgrade to SQL Server 2022 (mainstream support until Jan 2033)
alternatives.warning: 🚫 Extended Support ends July 2026. This is the only long-term fix.

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2019
alternatives.action: Upgrade to SQL Server 2019 as an interim step if 2022 migration is not yet feasible
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ SQL Server 2019 mainstream support ended Feb 2025. Target 2022 for long-term support.

Internet Explorer (All Versions).label: Internet Explorer
Internet Explorer (All Versions).action: Remove IE and migrate to Microsoft Edge (Chromium)
Internet Explorer (All Versions).warning: 🚫 Internet Explorer is fully retired. No security patches. Immediate action required.

alternatives.label: IE Mode (Edge)
alternatives.action: Deploy Edge with IE Mode for legacy intranet sites that require IE rendering engine
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ IE Mode in Edge maintains the IE rendering engine for compatibility. Full IE removal is still the goal.
